title: Mumbai Local Minimum Fare Path
description: The Mumbai suburban network is modeled as `n` stations (0..n-1) connected by `edges` where each edge is `[u, v, fare]` (bidirectional, non-negative fare in rupees). Given a `src` and `dst` station, return the minimum total fare from `src` to `dst`. If `dst` is unreachable, return `-1`.
hints: Classic Dijkstra — non-negative weights pe sabse fast.
MinHeap inline likh — JS me built-in priority queue nahi hai.
Visited check heap pop ke baad lagana, push pe nahi (stale entries skip ho jaayein).

title: Razorpay Payment Routing Minimum Cost
description: Razorpay routes payments through `n` payment gateways (0..n-1). Directed edges `[u, v, cost]` mean a payment can flow from gateway u to v at the listed cost. Given source `src` and target `dst`, return the minimum total cost to route a payment, or `-1` if unreachable. Costs are non-negative.
hints: Directed graph — sirf u->v wali entry add kar.
Dijkstra with min-heap.
src===dst → 0 immediately.

title: Swiggy Delivery Shortest With Traffic
description: Swiggy's routing engine receives a network of `n` road junctions (0..n-1) with bidirectional `edges = [u, v, minutes]`. Some roads may have traffic delays modeled as positive minutes. Given `src` (rider) and `dst` (customer), return the minimum total minutes for the delivery. Return `-1` if unreachable.
hints: Bidirectional edges → both directions me add.
Dijkstra hi best — non-negative weights.
src===dst → 0.

title: Indian Railways Cheapest Route With K Stops
description: Indian Railways has `n` cities (0..n-1) connected by directed `flights = [u, v, price]`. From `src` to `dst`, find the cheapest fare using AT MOST `k` intermediate stops (so at most k+1 edges). Return `-1` if no such route exists.
hints: Bellman-Ford k+1 iterations — clean fit.
Har iteration me 'previous' distance array se relax kar — current me update karna race condition deta hai.
Initial dist[src]=0, rest Infinity.

title: PhonePe Network Delay Broadcast
description: PhonePe broadcasts a config update from server `k` to all `n` servers (1..n) over directed links `times = [u, v, w]` (w = milliseconds). Return the minimum time it takes for ALL servers to receive the update. If some server is unreachable, return `-1`.
hints: Dijkstra from k. Answer = max(dist[1..n]).
1-indexed nodes — internal me 0-index ya direct work karega.
Koi unreachable mila → -1.

title: Infosys Detect Negative Cost Cycle
description: An Infosys arbitrage detector models currency conversions as a directed graph with `n` currencies (0..n-1) and edges `[u, v, w]` where w is a (possibly negative) cost. Return `true` if a negative-weight cycle is reachable from node `0`, otherwise `false`.
hints: Bellman-Ford classic — n-1 iterations relax. Ek aur iteration me update hua → negative cycle.
Sirf reachable-from-0 cycle count karna — initial dist[0]=0 rest Infinity.
Infinity+w skip kar — JS me Infinity arithmetic safe but cleaner.

title: TCS All-Pairs Shortest Path Matrix
description: TCS infra team needs the shortest distance between every pair of `n` data centers. Given `n` and directed `edges = [u, v, w]` with non-negative weights, return an `n x n` matrix `dist` where `dist[i][j]` = shortest distance from i to j (use `-1` if unreachable; `dist[i][i] = 0`).
hints: Floyd-Warshall — triple nested loop k,i,j.
Initialize dist[i][i]=0, dist[u][v]=w for edges, else Infinity.
End me Infinity ko -1 me convert kar.

title: Flipkart Fulfillment Network MST (Prim)
description: Flipkart wants to lay fiber connecting `n` warehouses (0..n-1) with minimum total cable cost. Given undirected `edges = [u, v, cost]`, return the total cost of the minimum spanning tree. If the graph is disconnected, return `-1`.
hints: Prim's: heap se min-edge nikaal jo MST tree extend kare.
Visited array maintain — visited node ki edges skip kar.
n nodes connect → n-1 edges in MST. Kam mile → disconnected.

title: Ola Fleet MST (Kruskal Union-Find)
description: Ola plans charging cables between `n` parking hubs. Given undirected `edges = [u, v, cost]`, return the minimum spanning tree cost using Kruskal's algorithm. Return `-1` if the graph is disconnected.
hints: Edges sort kar by cost ascending.
Union-Find inline class — union karte waqt cycle avoid.
n-1 edges accept hone chahiye, warna disconnected.

title: IndiGo Reconstruct Flight Itinerary
description: IndiGo gives you a list of one-way `tickets` represented as `[from, to]` airport codes (lowercase strings). Reconstruct the itinerary in order, starting from `"blr"` (Bengaluru). All tickets must be used exactly once. If multiple valid itineraries exist, return the lexicographically smallest one.
constraints: Always at least one valid itinerary
hints: Hierholzer's Eulerian path algorithm.
Har source ki destination list ko sort kar; queue me se shift karke smallest pick.
DFS post-order push, end me reverse — mature trick.

title: Amazon Alien Dictionary Order
description: Amazon discovered a sorted list of `words` from an alien language using lowercase English letters. Derive the alien alphabet order. Return any valid order as a string. If the input is inconsistent, return `""`. If multiple orders are valid, return the lexicographically smallest one (use a min-heap when picking the next 0-indegree letter).
hints: Adjacent word pairs me first differing char ka edge nikaal — u before v.
Topological sort with min-heap → lex smallest.
Prefix invalid case: 'abc' before 'ab' → return "".

title: IIT Network Articulation Points
description: An IIT campus network has `n` switches (0..n-1) connected by undirected `edges`. Find all articulation points — switches whose removal would split the network into more components. Return the list of articulation points sorted ascending.
hints: Tarjan's lowlink — disc[u], low[u] maintain.
Root special: 2+ DFS children → articulation. Non-root: koi child v with low[v] >= disc[u] → articulation.
Parent ko skip karna while DFS recursion.

title: Google Critical Connections (Bridges)
description: Given an undirected network of `n` servers and `connections` (edges), return all critical connections — edges whose removal would disconnect the graph (bridges). Return each bridge with the smaller endpoint first, sorted lexicographically (by first endpoint, then second).
hints: Tarjan's bridge: edge (u,v) is bridge iff low[v] > disc[u] (DFS tree edge).
Multiple parent-edge handling: agar duplicate parent edge ho toh tricky — yahan no duplicates per constraints.
Output format: [min, max] each pair, fir overall sort.

title: LinkedIn Strongly Connected Components
description: LinkedIn models a follow graph as a directed graph with `n` users and edges `[u, v]` meaning u follows v. Return the number of strongly connected components (SCCs) — maximal subsets where every pair is mutually reachable.
hints: Kosaraju 2-pass: original graph DFS to fill stack by finish time.
Reverse graph; pop stack, DFS each unvisited — each DFS = one SCC.
Tarjan's also works in 1 pass with stack + lowlink.

title: Uber Find Redundant Connection
description: Uber added an extra cable to its `n`-node network, turning a tree into a graph with exactly one cycle. Given the `edges` (1-indexed, in the order they were added), return the LAST edge that completes a cycle. Each edge is `[u, v]`. Exactly one such edge exists.
hints: Union-Find: edges in order, jab union fail kare wahi answer.
n = edges.length (tree me n-1 edges, plus 1 extra).
1-indexed nodes — UF size n+1.

title: IISc Critical Edges In MST
description: Given `n` nodes and an undirected weighted edge list `edges` (with original index), return the sorted list of edge indices that are CRITICAL — i.e., appear in every MST. An edge is critical iff removing it strictly increases the MST cost (or makes the graph disconnected).
hints: Reference MST cost compute kar (Kruskal).
Har edge ke liye usko skip karke MST recompute — agar cost greater ya disconnected → critical.
Edge index sorted ascending result.

title: OYO Swim In Rising Water Grid
description: An OYO flood-rescue grid is `n x n`, where `grid[i][j]` is the elevation. Water rises continuously; at time `t`, you can swim into any cell with elevation <= t. Starting at `(0,0)` at time 0, return the minimum time to reach `(n-1,n-1)`. You may move 4-directionally.
hints: Dijkstra variant: edge 'cost' = max(current time, neighbor elevation).
Min-heap pe pop kar smallest accumulated max.
Visited check — pop ke baad.

title: Paytm Path With Maximum Success Probability
description: Paytm's payment retry network has `n` nodes (0..n-1) and undirected `edges = [u, v]` with parallel `succProb` array — `succProb[i]` is the probability that an attempt over `edges[i]` succeeds. Return the maximum end-to-end success probability from `start` to `end`. If no path exists, return 0.0.
hints: Dijkstra-like but with MAX-heap on probability — multiplication of probs.
Visited-once: pop ke baad mark, future smaller probs skip.
start===end → 1.0.

title: BYJUS Path With Minimum Effort
description: BYJU's hiking simulator gives a `rows x cols` grid `heights`. Effort along a path = the maximum absolute difference between consecutive cells. Starting at `(0,0)`, return the minimum effort path to `(rows-1, cols-1)`. Move 4-directionally.
hints: Dijkstra: edge weight = |height[a] - height[b]|; path weight = max along path.
Min-heap on running max.
Single cell: 0 effort.

title: Freshworks Team Bipartite Check
description: Freshworks wants to split `n` employees (0..n-1) into two teams such that every conflict edge in `dislikes = [a, b]` puts the two endpoints in DIFFERENT teams. Return `true` if such a split (2-coloring / bipartite) is possible, else `false`.
hints: BFS/DFS coloring: 0 ya 1 assign kar, neighbors ko opposite.
Disconnected components ke liye loop over all unvisited nodes.
Conflict mile (same color) → return false.
